profile.description: Your name, email, password, and two-factor auth live on your account profile.

target.title: Execution target
target.description: Where runs execute — the server, or a paired Local Agent on your machine

authoring.title: Authoring mode
authoring.description: How approved cases become Playwright specs — generate blind then heal, or drive the app live via browser-harness to capture real selectors

heal.title: Self-heal mode
heal.description: How a failing spec is repaired — classic generates a fix and re-runs, or live drives the app via browser-harness to reproduce and fix from the real DOM (needs a paired local agent)

liveBudget.title: Live session budget
liveBudget.description: Max Claude spend (USD) per live browser-harness session — shared by live authoring and live self-heal. Raise it if a session needs to create test data and drive a long flow before it runs out.

authoringLog.title: Authoring log detail
authoringLog.description: How much of the live-authoring log to show — concise shows only Claude's readable narration, verbose also shows the raw browser-harness/Bash commands

parallel.title: Parallel workers
parallel.description: Default up to {{n}} cases at once per Run

maxCases.title: Max test cases per ticket
maxCases.description: Cap AI generation to at most {{n}} cases per ticket

retryFlaky.title: Auto-retry flaky tests
retryFlaky.description: Retry failed cases up to 2 times

screenshotOnFail.title: Screenshot on failure
screenshotOnFail.description: Capture full-page evidence for every failed step

autoAnnotate.title: Auto-annotate failure screenshots
autoAnnotate.description: Runs a Claude vision analysis on each failed screenshot to draw the problem area — one AI call per failure

video.title: Record video
video.description: Save an MP4 of each run (uses more storage)

headless.title: Run browser headless
headless.description: Execute Playwright without a visible browser window (turn off to watch runs)

specGate.title: Gate generated specs
specGate.description: Block specs with placeholders, invented routes/selectors or flaky patterns, and run the AI spec review. Turn off to accept every generated spec as runnable without gating.

model.label: Claude model
model.hint: Model used for all AI actions (analysis, generation, self-heal).

budget.label: Weekly token budget (tokens)
budget.hint: Shown as a usage bar in the Claude stats panel. 0 = no budget.

concurrency.label: Ticket concurrency
concurrency.hint: How many tickets in a run are analyzed & generated in parallel. Higher is faster but uses more of your Claude rate window; values above 1 only take effect on Postgres.

overrides.label: Per-action model overrides
overrides.hint: Override the Claude model for a specific AI action. Leave on "Inherit" to use each action's default (mechanical actions default to Haiku; the rest use the Claude model above).

background.title: 3D background
background.description: Animated neural-constellation backdrop. Turn off for a flat background (lighter on the GPU).

providerGroup.deleteMessage: Delete "{{name}}"? Projects and tickets bound to it fall back to another connection of the same kind.

credential.tokenNotExposed: Not exposed by the API — the server never returns credential contents.
credential.switchToOwn: The admin rotates and maintains this token. Switch to Your own account to use a personal plan instead.

card.intro: Q‑Agent authenticates the Claude CLI with the token inside a .credentials.json file. Use the shared account your admin maintains, or upload your own personal Claude account.
card.sharedDescription: Managed by your workspace admin. Nothing to set up — you're ready to run.
card.ownDescription: Upload your personal .credentials.json to use your own Claude plan.
card.noShared: Your admin hasn't set up a shared Claude account yet. Ask them to add one from Admin › Claude credentials, or upload your own below.
card.credentialsSaveFailed: Could not save that credentials file
card.credentialsRemoved: Removed your personal credentials — now using the shared account

dropzone.browse: or click to browse · found at ~/.claude/.credentials.json

users.notAuthorized: User management is available to workspace administrators only. If you need access, ask an admin to change your role.
users.title: User management
users.actionsSelf: You can't manage your own account here
users.removeMessage: {{name}} will lose access to the workspace immediately. This cannot be undone.

invite.title: Invite a user
invite.subtitle: They'll get an email to join the Surency workspace.
invite.roleHint: Admins can manage users and shared Claude credentials.

create.title: Create a user
create.subtitle: Sets up the account directly with a password — no invite email.

adminCredentials.notAuthorized: Claude credentials are managed by workspace administrators only. If you need access, ask an admin to change your role.
adminCredentials.title: Claude credentials
adminCredentials.intro: These are the shared Claude accounts your team runs on. Upload and maintain the .credentials.json here — Q‑Agent writes the token to ~/.claude/.credentials.json and every member on the shared account uses it. Members can also bring their own from Settings › Claude account.
adminCredentials.noneConfigured: No shared Claude account configured yet.
adminCredentials.addDescription: Upload a .credentials.json exported from an authenticated Claude CLI
adminCredentials.removeMessage: Every member relying on the shared account (anyone who hasn't uploaded their own credentials) will lose AI access until a new one is added.
adminCredentials.saveFailed: Could not save that credentials file
adminCredentials.removeFailed: Failed to remove the shared credential

sharedWorkspace.notAuthorized: The shared workspace is managed by workspace administrators only. If you need access, ask an admin to change your role.
sharedWorkspace.title: Shared workspace
sharedWorkspace.intro: Projects here live in the shared namespace — members can clone them into their own workspace instead of rebuilding knowledge from scratch. Open a project to add a repository and settings, then build its knowledge; a cloned project drops its provider-connection bindings, so members re-bind their own connections after cloning.
sharedWorkspace.loadFailed: Failed to load the shared catalog
sharedWorkspace.empty: No shared projects yet. Create one to get started.
sharedWorkspace.noRepo: No repository configured — open settings to add one before building knowledge.

create.title: New shared project
create.subtitle: Creates the shell — you'll add the repo and settings next.
create.keyHint: Stays unchanged when a member clones it — pick something stable.

sharedProject.notAuthorized: The shared workspace is managed by workspace administrators only.
sharedProject.description: Settings members inherit when they clone. Connections here are used only to build shared knowledge — they are dropped on clone, so members re-bind their own.
